specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a desktop code review app with CI/CD integration, inspired by a project built with Electron, React, and Tailwind CSS. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a pull request list panel with title, author, and status, a diff viewer with added lines highlighted green and removed lines highlighted red, a CI pipeline status column showing pass and fail badges per check, an inline comment thread that opens below a diff line, a review submit panel with Approve and Request Changes buttons, and a GitHub connection status badge.

followUps.label: add-timeline-view
followUps.prompt: Add a PR timeline view showing commit history, review events, and comments in chronological order below the diff.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a Git worktree workspace manager, inspired by a tool that treats worktrees as daily-driver workspaces. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a worktree list panel showing branch name, local path, and status for each worktree, a create new worktree form with branch name and base branch fields, a switch active worktree button per row, a health indicator that flags stale or diverged worktrees, a linked branch remote status, and a terminal command snippet panel showing the git worktree add and switch commands.

followUps.label: add-prune-action
followUps.prompt: Add a prune action that lists orphaned worktrees whose branches have been deleted and offers to remove them in one click.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a secret management dashboard using the SecretFetch approach. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a secret catalog table with masked values and a reveal-on-hover toggle, source tabs for env file, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, and GCP Secret Manager, a validation status badge per secret showing present or missing, a batch load button, a last-refreshed timestamp per secret, and a code panel showing the SecretFetch Go struct tag annotation syntax.

followUps.label: add-rotation-reminder
followUps.prompt: Add a rotation reminder panel that flags secrets older than 90 days with a warning badge and a rotate button.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of an MCP smart tool router explorer. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a tool catalog panel listing at least eight mock MCP tools with names, descriptions, and categories, a prompt input that routes the query to the best matching tool based on intent keywords, a routing confidence score bar, a top-three candidate list, a context rot warning panel that activates when more than ten tools are loaded, and a tool filter by category.

followUps.label: add-tool-audit-log
followUps.prompt: Add a tool audit log panel that records each routing decision with the prompt, selected tool, and confidence score.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a simple socket programming library demo using a clean API called Conduit. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a connection panel with host and port fields and a connect button, a message send area, a live received messages feed that updates as messages arrive, a connected clients count badge, an error display panel, a broadcast versus direct message toggle, and a code panel showing the Conduit API with simple open, send, listen, and close calls.

followUps.label: add-ping-latency
followUps.prompt: Add a ping latency panel that sends a heartbeat every two seconds and displays round-trip time.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a semantic versioning automation dashboard. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a mock commit history panel that classifies each commit as feat, fix, chore, or breaking change using colour badges, a version bump calculator showing the current version and the computed next version, a changelog preview panel with grouped release notes, a GitHub Actions pipeline integration diagram, manual override buttons for major, minor, and patch bumps, and a dry run toggle that previews all changes without applying them.

followUps.label: add-tag-preview
followUps.prompt: Add a Git tag preview panel showing the exact tag command that would be run and the resulting tag name.
